title: APR Committee
description: Jointly established by New York University and East China Normal University, NYU Shanghai is the third degree-granting campus in NYU’s global network, joining NYU Abu Dhabi and NYU's campus in New York City.
text:
APR Committee
The APR Committee acts as a facilitator in the APR process by providing support to academic units under review. Committee membership includes faculty appointed to 2-year terms by the Deans of Arts & Sciences, Computer Science, and Business, and additional faculty elected to 3-year terms by the Faculty Council. The terms are staggered to provide continuity via experienced committee members. The responsibilities of the Committee are procedural, not evaluative, and they include:
Establishing the cycle of reviews and assisting the Provost’s Office in ensuring academic units are informed of upcoming reviews
Ensuring that academic units have access to the data necessary for preparing the self-study
Providing feedback on self-studies before the external review
Overseeing the external review
Preparing an executive summary for the Provost based on the report from the external review, the response from the academic unit, and the Dean’s response
Instigating a review of APR policies and procedures at the end of the first 7-year cycle
APR Coordinator Assistant Dean for Academic Affairs
